metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strike the `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` reference in the Approach section's description bullet and replace it with the `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` mechanism described in the remediation, so the Approach and the remediation are self-consistent.
evidence: Approach: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text'

Remediation: 'FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description is the SEND-path sanitizer: it fits AND emits a logger.warning on truncation, which is wrong for a read-only diagnostic. Instead, mirror the canonical differ … OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote'
The Approach section body was not updated after the remediation was appended, leaving directly contradictory instructions in the same plan.
findings.finding: The Approach section still prescribes `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` for description comparison, but the Plan-review remediation section explicitly rejects this in favor of `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` — an implementer reading only the Approach section would use the wrong mechanism.
findings.location: Approach section (description bullet) vs Plan-review remediation finding #2
scenarios: An implementer follows the Approach section literally and uses sanitize_description, passing behavior tests (which target the correct path) only if the tests are written first — otherwise the wrong implementation ships.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach description bullet, replace 'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance' with 'fit the local description through `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_desc}, ticket=ticket).get("description")` (no warning), compare with rstrip tolerance (`_text_matches`) against the mapper-decoded canonical remote description — mirroring outbound_field_diff.py:270-279'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the Approach that ALL field reads in `_compare_pair` (including title/summary and labels) are fed from the `canonical_remote` dict (output of `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local`), not from the raw `jira_issue` argument, so the implementation is consistently canonicalized end-to-end.
evidence: Plan Approach lists only three canonicalization targets: 'assignee: neutral membership matching...', 'description: compare the port-sanitized local text...', 'status/priority: compare the local value against the canonically mapped remote value' — title and labels are not mentioned.
reconcile_check.py:204-205: `local_title = local_ticket.get('title', local_ticket.get('summary')); jira_summary = jira_issue.get('summary', jira_issue.get('title'))` — reads raw jira_issue directly
reconcile_check.py:262-266: `_compare_labels(local_ticket.get('tags'), jira_issue.get('labels'))` — reads raw jira_issue.get('labels') directly
inbound_fields.py:192-193: `if 'summary' in jira_fields: out['title'] = ...` — canonical dict provides 'title'
625b ticket step 4c: 'The Jira map_remote_to_local output gains labels: list[str]' — canonical dict provides 'labels'
AC1 gate only checks for adapters/jira/ path literals and import statements — it cannot enforce which dict (raw vs canonical) field reads come from
findings.finding: The Approach section specifies canonicalization for assignee/description/status/priority but is silent on what happens to the title comparison (currently reads `jira_issue.get('summary')` from the raw dict) and the labels comparison (`_compare_labels` reads `jira_issue.get('labels')` from the raw dict). After calling `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_issue)` once per pair, the canonical dict will carry `title` (from `summary`) and `labels`. An implementer who feeds these two paths from the raw dict rather than the canonical dict will produce an inconsistent half-canonicalized `_compare_pair` — and the AC1 gate (which only prohibits `adapters/jira/` path literals and imports) will not catch it. The plan should explicitly state that ALL field reads in `_compare_pair` use the canonical remote dict, not the raw `jira_issue` argument.
findings.location: Approach section, bullet points for assignee/description/status/priority; reconcile_check.py:204-215 (title/summary) and :261-266 (labels)
scenarios: An implementer calls map_remote_to_local once to get canonical_remote, uses canonical_remote for the four COMPARABLE_FIELDS, but leaves title and labels reading from the raw jira_issue argument — the gate stays green but the 'canonicalize once' principle is violated and the implementation is internally inconsistent.

If a future backend stores labels in a nested shape (e.g. {name: 'team:backend'} objects), `jira_issue.get('labels')` would return the nested form while the canonical dict provides a flat list — a correctness bug that the plan's current spec would not catch.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Approach: 'After calling `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_issue)` once per bound pair, ALL field reads in `_compare_pair` use the resulting canonical dict: title from `canonical_remote["title"]`, labels from `canonical_remote.get("labels", [])`, and the four COMPARABLE_FIELDS from their canonical equivalents. The raw `jira_issue` argument is retained only to supply the raw `jira_value` in the discrepancy record (unchanged).'

findings.finding: The plan asserts description comparison 'mirrors the differ's description parity rule' by using `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`, but the actual canonical differ (`outbound_field_diff.py:275-279`) fits descriptions via `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({'description': local_desc})` — which additionally serializes to ADF format — not via `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` alone. An executor following the plan literally will wire a different operation, breaking the stated decision-equivalence for ADF-heavy descriptions.
findings.location: Approach section — description bullet: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance, mirroring the differ's description parity rule [rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f]'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`)' with 'fit the local description via `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_val})` (identical to `outbound_field_diff.py:275-279`) and compare the fitted text against the mapper-decoded remote text with rstrip tolerance — do NOT use `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` directly, which skips ADF serialization.'

findings.finding: The plan specifies `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` for the local-description side of the description comparison, but this is the SEND-PATH sanitizer: `_sanitize_description` calls `fit_text_to_adf_limit` AND emits `logger.warning` on truncation. Using it in a read-only diagnostic tool introduces spurious send-side warning logs every time reconcile-check runs on a long description, and creates a different comparison surface than what `outbound_field_diff.diff_canonical_fields` uses (`outbound_mapper.map_fields_to_remote({'description': local_desc})`, which applies the ADF fit silently). The implementer will face an ambiguous choice between the two paths.
findings.location: Approach section — description canonicalization bullet: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description) against the mapper-decoded remote text'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description)' with 'compare `inbound_mapper.normalize_rich_text(local_val)` fitted via `adf.fit_text_to_adf_limit` against `canonical_remote["description"]` (already decoded by `map_remote_to_local`)' — this is exactly the `outbound_field_diff._text_matches(fitted, canonical_remote.get("description", ""))` path and stays warning-free.

findings.finding: `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` is documented as a 'send-side only' sanitizer and emits a WARNING log on truncation; the inbound differ — the stated parity reference — uses `_load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit` directly (inbound_differ.py:210), NOT `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`. Using the send-side sanitizer in reconcile_check's comparison path would introduce spurious warning logs on every reconcile-check run for any description near the ADF limit, contradicting the 'no side-effects' expectation of a pure comparison.
findings.location: Approach section — 'description: compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text'
findings.suggested_fix: In the description comparison, call `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_issue)["description"]` for the remote side and use `fit_text_to_adf_limit(str(local_val or ""))` (via the same lazy adf loader the inbound differ uses) for the local side, preserving the rstrip tolerance — matching the inbound differ pattern at inbound_differ.py:210.

findings.finding: The description comparison plan — 'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance' — is an unverified behavioral equivalence claim. `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` is the send-side truncator (`_sanitize_description` → `fit_text_to_adf_limit`); after canonicalization via `map_remote_to_local`, the remote text is already plain text (ADF decoded, no fit applied). Today's code applies `fit_text_to_adf_limit` to the LOCAL value and compares against the ADF-decoded REMOTE value. The new path applies `sanitize_description` (= fit) to the local value and compares against the mapper-decoded remote value — which is structurally the same, BUT `sanitize_description` also enforces the description-must-be-str contract and emits a warning, making it a side-effectful call on the read path. The plan does not acknowledge this distinction.
findings.location: Approach section — 'description: compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `backend.sanitizer`'s underlying pure fit (e.g. call `adf.fit_text_to_adf_limit` via a port helper, or access it through the mapper's decoded output directly with rstrip) rather than `sanitize_description`. Alternatively, expose a separate `FieldSanitizer.fit_description(description: str) -> str` method that is pure (no warning, no raise) for comparison use.

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: improve
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_reconcile_check.py` in Scope as a required update target, and specify how existing live-shape test fixtures (e.g. `_live_binding()`, `test_normalized_agree_raw_differ_counts_in_sync`) will be adapted to inject a mapper stub (or a Jira-aware `FakeInboundMapper` that handles nested shapes) so that the existing tests pass under the new injection seam.
evidence: test_reconcile_check.py:354-376: test_normalized_agree_raw_differ_counts_in_sync and test_real_status_drift_still_reported call rc_mod.reconcile_check() with raw Jira shapes (nested status/priority objects, ADF dict, assignee dict) and no mapper arguments
test_reconcile_check.py:34-36: rc_mod fixture loads reconcile_check.py via spec_from_file_location with no backend configuration
Plan Approach: 'injection seam (optional inbound_mapper / outbound_mapper params defaulting to the lazy select_backend resolve)' — the lazy default will attempt load_config() during test execution

backend_support.py:100-107: _FakeInbound.map_remote_to_local passes through scalars directly and does not handle Jira nested shapes (status={"name":"To Do"}, ADF dicts, assignee dicts), so existing live-shape fixtures cannot simply be re-used with FakeInbound
Scope section names only 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (reconcile-check comparison behavior tests)' without explicitly enumerating the existing test_reconcile_check.py as requiring update
findings.finding: The existing `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_reconcile_check.py` is an unenumerated consumer that will break after the injection seam is added. It directly calls `rc_mod.reconcile_check(local_tickets, jira_snapshot, store)` with raw Jira-shaped snapshot values (nested `{"name":...}` status/priority objects, ADF description dicts, assignee dicts — see `_live_binding()` fixture and `test_normalized_agree_raw_differ_counts_in_sync`) and passes NO `inbound_mapper`/`outbound_mapper` arguments. Once `_compare_pair` resolves mappers via `select_backend(load_config())` on the default path, these tests will fail at runtime because no backend is configured in the test environment. The plan's scope says 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (reconcile-check comparison behavior tests)' but does not name this existing file as a required update target.
scenarios: Executor adds injection seam to _compare_pair; existing test_normalized_agree_raw_differ_counts_in_sync calls reconcile_check() without mapper args; default path calls select_backend(load_config()) which fails because no backend is registered in test environment; CI breaks on the existing test file that the plan did not enumerate for update.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_reconcile_check.py` to the Scope list and note that the live-shape tests must be updated to pass an injected `inbound_mapper` / `outbound_mapper` (e.g. a thin Jira-aware stub or the real `JiraInbound` configured with test env vars) rather than relying on the `select_backend(load_config())` default path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the original AC2 text to explicitly exclude the annotation-label case (e.g., '…identical to today's for Jira across the Testing cases, except annotation-label status precedence which is an intended improvement per the remediation'), so the criterion and the remediation scope note are consistent.
evidence: AC2: 'Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira across the Testing cases (including the runny-lens-strafe live-shape regression)'
Remediation: 'AC2's "identical decisions" is therefore scoped to exclude this one annotation-label case, which is recorded as an INTENDED improvement … not a regression'
The original AC2 text remains verbatim unchanged while the remediation adds a scope exclusion, creating two inconsistent authoritative statements about AC2's meaning.
findings.finding: Original AC2 says 'Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira across the Testing cases' without qualification, but the remediation section explicitly carves out the annotation-label case as an intended deviation — the AC2 text itself is never updated, leaving a direct contradiction between the original criterion and the remediation scope note.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / AC2 vs Plan-review remediation finding #3
scenarios: A test author treating AC2 as authoritative writes a test asserting annotation-label tickets produce the same (wrong) comparison as the old code, causing the intentional improvement to be incorrectly rejected.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: '(exception: tickets carrying a `rebar-status:blocked`/`cancelled` annotation label may yield a different — corrected — local status via canonical mapper precedence; this is the one intended improvement, not a regression)'. This makes the third added AC bullet a verification of AC2's own stated exception rather than a contradictory addendum.

findings.finding: AC2 states 'Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira across the Testing cases … including the runny-lens-strafe live-shape regression.' However, for tickets with `rebar-status:blocked` or `rebar-status:cancelled` annotation labels, the NEW canonical approach will produce DIFFERENT (more correct) decisions than today: `inbound_fields._map_jira_to_local_fields` (ticket 929a) maps these annotation labels to `blocked`/`cancelled` local status, so `blocked` local == `blocked` canonical (no discrepancy). Today's code reads raw `status.name` via `_extract_jira_field` and `_values_match_with_mapping`, mapping `blocked` local → `In Progress` Jira — a false discrepancy. The AC's 'identical to today's' language creates a Testing trap where the characterization suite may be written to expect the old (wrong) behavior for annotation-label statuses.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2; Testing — 'Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add deletion of `_values_match_with_mapping`, `_status_maps()`, `_STATUS_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`/`_STATUS_JIRA_TO_LOCAL` globals, `_PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, `_TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, and the `_load_config()` loader to the Approach's deletion list, since all become unreachable dead code when status/priority comparison is replaced by the canonical mapper.
evidence: reconcile_check.py:87-99: _STATUS_LOCAL_TO_JIRA/_STATUS_JIRA_TO_LOCAL globals and _status_maps() — only called by _values_match_with_mapping
reconcile_check.py:103-117: _PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA and _TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA dicts — only consumed by _values_match_with_mapping
reconcile_check.py:153-178: _values_match_with_mapping — the plan replaces its call site at :247-248 with canonical mapper comparison
reconcile_check.py:46-48: _load_config() — only called by _status_maps(); if _status_maps() is deleted, _load_config() becomes dead
Plan Approach: 'Delete the _load_outbound_fields/_load_adf sibling loads' — does not mention these companion helpers
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate deletion of now-dead helpers `_values_match_with_mapping`, `_status_maps()`, `_PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, and `_TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA` from `reconcile_check.py`. After the canonical-mapper approach is adopted for status/priority comparison (replacing `_extract_jira_field` + `_values_match_with_mapping` at lines 247-248), these four helpers and their associated config dependency (`_load_config()`) become entirely unreachable dead code. The plan's Approach says to delete `_load_outbound_fields`/`_load_adf` but omits these internal helpers.
scenarios: Executor deletes _load_outbound_fields/_load_adf as specified but leaves _values_match_with_mapping and _status_maps() as dead code; reconcile_check.py passes the neutrality gate but carries unreachable config-reading helpers that mislead future readers and fail lint/dead-code checks.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, extend the deletion bullet to: 'Delete `_load_outbound_fields`/`_load_adf` sibling loads, `_values_match_with_mapping`, `_status_maps()`, `_PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, `_TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, and `_load_config()` — all become unreachable after the canonical-mapper comparison replaces their call sites.'

findings.finding: The plan's Context and Approach explicitly state that the PRIMARY motivation is fixing non-Jira backend correctness ('its discrepancy scan would mis-flag every binding on a non-Jira backend'), yet no Acceptance Criterion covers or verifies this non-Jira behavior — AC2 only requires 'identical to today's for Jira'. The non-Jira correctness fix is described work with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / AC2 vs. Context ('second-backend correctness hole') and Approach ('Rejected alternative')
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2 with a second bullet: 'For a synthetic non-Jira backend (stub InboundMapper returning canonical local-vocabulary values), a fully-in-sync bound pair yields zero discrepancies — verified by an additional behavior test in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ using a mock backend.'

findings.finding: The Testing section exercises only Jira-shaped payloads to verify behavioral correctness, but the core motivation of this story is fixing non-Jira backends (the runny-lens-strafe failure mode on non-Jira bindings). The extended neutrality gate is a structural/AST check (import scan), not a behavioral test. No test specifies a non-Jira mock backend where a synced pair produces zero discrepancies or a divergent pair is correctly flagged — so the actual bug fix (non-Jira correctness) is never behaviorally exercised.
findings.suggested_fix: In `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/`, add a parameterized or separate test fixture that wires a trivial stub backend (e.g., a `StubInboundMapper` that maps `{"summary": ..., "assignee": ..., "status": {"name": ...}}` to local vocabulary with different key names than Jira), feeds reconcile_check a live-shaped snapshot from that backend, and asserts zero discrepancies for a synced pair and a reported discrepancy for a divergent field. Add this scenario to the Testing section and AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly in the description-comparison step what object is passed as `ticket` to `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` within reconcile_check's bound-pair loop (e.g. 'the local `Ticket` object from the bound pair, the same type used in outbound_differ').
evidence: 'fit the local description via the injected `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_desc}, ticket=ticket)`' — `ticket` provenance unstated
reconcile_check.py iterates bound pairs; executor must infer which object to pass as `ticket`
findings.finding: The plan specifies `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_desc}, ticket=ticket)` for the description ADF-fit inside reconcile_check, but does not state what `ticket` refers to in reconcile_check's loop context — the local rebar ticket object from the bound pair being checked. An executor implementing the injection must know whether `ticket` is the local ticket, the binding object, or something else, and the plan never makes this explicit.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation section, item 2: description comparison reuses 625b's parity
scenarios: Executor passes the wrong object as `ticket` (e.g. the binding record rather than the local ticket), causing silent mismatch in ADF-fit behavior
If `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote` raises on an unexpected `ticket` type, the bug surfaces only at runtime
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'Here `ticket` is the local `Ticket` object from the bound pair being checked — the same type `outbound_differ` passes to `map_fields_to_remote`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an annotation-label edge case to the Testing section (e.g., 'annotation-label status precedence: a ticket with rebar-status:blocked/cancelled maps to the correct canonical local status, not the raw Jira workflow status') so the Testing section enumerates all cases verified by the ACs.
evidence: Testing section lists: happy path, edge/failure (assignee, ADF, divergent field, unmapped remote status), gate — annotation-label case is absent.

Third added AC: 'A ticket with a rebar-status:blocked/cancelled annotation label reconciles to the correct local status (annotation-label precedence via the canonical mapper) — verified by a behavior test'
findings.finding: The Testing section does not list the annotation-label scenario as a test case, but the third added AC in the remediation explicitly requires a behavior test for it ('A ticket with a rebar-status:blocked/cancelled annotation label reconciles to the correct local status … verified by a behavior test') — the Testing and Acceptance Criteria sections are inconsistent.
findings.location: Testing section vs Plan-review remediation / third added AC bullet
scenarios: A developer writing tests uses the Testing section as the complete specification and omits the annotation-label behavior test, causing the third added AC to fail at review time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend the second added AC) to verify that `reconcile_check`'s comparison path accepts optional `inbound_mapper`/`outbound_mapper` injection params and that the unit behavior tests exercise these params directly (not via live `select_backend` resolution).
evidence: Remediation: 'An injection seam (optional `inbound_mapper` / `outbound_mapper` params defaulting to the lazy `select_backend` resolve, mirroring outbound_differ / outbound_comments) keeps the spec-loaded unit tests working'
No AC mentions the injection seam parameters, their defaults, or their correctness — AC1 covers import hygiene, AC2 covers decision parity, AC3 covers lint/test green.
findings.finding: The remediation describes a new injection seam ('optional `inbound_mapper` / `outbound_mapper` params defaulting to the lazy `select_backend` resolve') as a deliverable on reconcile_check.py, but no AC verifies that this seam exists or is correctly wired — it is uncovered work.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation finding #2 vs Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: An implementer omits the injection seam (e.g., hard-codes the live backend), the unit tests still pass because they work around it, but the seam is absent and the design does not mirror outbound_differ as intended.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief architectural note (ADR entry, AGENTS.md section, or inline doc-comment in reconcile_check.py) explaining why reconcile_check requires BOTH InboundMapper and OutboundMapper — the only core module that does — and how the injection seam supports backend-neutral testing.
evidence: 'reconcile_check therefore resolves BOTH backend.inbound (canonicalize the snapshot) AND backend.outbound (description ADF-fit + resolve_assignee for the assignee identity) via select_backend(load_config())'
No mention of ADR or AGENTS.md/design-doc update anywhere in the plan
ABSENCE: no documentation update in Scope section (only reconcile_check.py, test_backend_neutrality.py, and tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ are listed)
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel dual-mapper dependency on reconcile_check.py (both InboundMapper AND OutboundMapper, unlike any other core module) but does not specify any ADR, AGENTS.md, or design-doc update to record this architectural decision for future maintainers.
scenarios: A future maintainer modifying reconcile_check assumes it only needs the inbound mapper (like the differ), adds a new field comparison using only InboundMapper, and silently breaks the outbound-path description/assignee logic.
A backend implementer adding a new backend wires InboundMapper but forgets OutboundMapper for reconcile_check, causing silent comparison failures on description and assignee fields.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level docstring or comment block in reconcile_check.py (and optionally AGENTS.md) stating: 'reconcile_check is the only core module that requires both InboundMapper (to canonicalize the remote snapshot) and OutboundMapper (to ADF-fit the local description and resolve assignee identity for comparison). Both are injected via optional parameters defaulting to select_backend(load_config()), mirroring the outbound_differ injection seam.'

findings.finding: The plan's AC1 promises a gate that fails if `reconcile_check.py` re-grows a 'by-path vendor sibling load' (`adapters/jira/...` path literal), but the existing `_all_imported_modules` AST walker in `test_backend_neutrality.py` only detects `import X` / `from X import ...` AST nodes — it cannot detect `_load_sibling('key', 'adapters/jira/adf.py')` path-string arguments. Adding `reconcile_check.py` to `_ZERO_ADAPTER_IMPORT_SCALAR_CORE` alone won't verify the path-literal constraint; the gate needs a separate path-string denylist scan.
findings.suggested_fix: In the extended neutrality test for reconcile_check.py, add: `text = (_REC / 'reconcile_check.py').read_text(); assert 'adapters/jira/' not in text, 'reconcile_check.py contains a by-path vendor sibling load'`. This mirrors the existing denylist pattern used for vendor snapshot key literals at test_backend_neutrality.py:178-190.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
